questions.question: Birds and mammals have feathers.
questions.explanation: Only birds have feathers. Mammals have fur or hair.

questions.question: Young mammals grow inside their mother's uterus.
Wild mammals are pets and farm animals.
questions.explanation: Pets and farm animals are domestic mammals, not wild.

pairs.right: ...is a domestic animal, it can live at home.
...lives on a farm and gives us eggs.

questions.question: Match each description to the correct part of a fish.
Adult amphibians lay soft eggs in water.
Adult amphibians live and grow in saltwater.
questions.explanation: Amphibians live in freshwater, not saltwater.

questions.question: Amphibians' bodies are covered in scales.
questions.explanation: Amphibians have smooth, moist skin, not scales.

questions.question: Young amphibians have fins and adults amphibians have legs.
All adult amphibians are herbivores.
questions.explanation: Most adult amphibians are carnivores, eating insects.

questions.question: Complete the sentences with the correct word from the box.
questions.sentence: Some reptiles have _____.	First, amphibians live in _____. Then, they live on _____.	Fish breathe with _____.	Young mammals feed on _____ from their mothers.	Birds use _____ to fly.	Animals that eat rabbits and chickens are _____.	Animals that eat plants are _____.	Animals that eat plants and animals are _____.	Dogs, cats and some cows are _____ animals.	Whales, tigers and eagles are _____ animals.

questions.question: Write the name of the ecosystem.
Classify the animals: reptiles or not reptiles.
